records.ety: [L. infans; pref. in- not + fari to speak: cf. F. enfant, whence OE. enfaunt. See Fame, and cf. Infante, Infanta.]

defs: A child in the first period of life, beginning at his birth; a young babe; sometimes, a child several years of age.
quotes: And tender cries of infants pierce the ear.

defs: A person who is not of full age, or who has not attained the age of legal capacity; a person under the age of twenty-one years; a minor.
notes: ☞ An infant under seven years of age is not penally responsible; between seven and fourteen years of age, he may be convicted of a malicious offense if malice be proved. He becomes of age on the day preceding his twenty-first birthday, previous to which time an infant has no capacity to contract.

defs: Of or pertaining to infancy, or the first period of life; tender; not mature; as, infant strength.
Intended for young children; as, an infant school.
To bear or bring forth, as a child; hence, to produce, in general.
quotes: This worthy motto, “No bishop, no king,” is . . . infanted out of the same fears.
